text.title: backup-recovery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-recovery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Restoring into a running production database without --clean and without --if-exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without --clean, existing tables accumulate duplicate data. Without --if-exists, restore fails on the first table that does not yet exist, leaving the database in a partial state.

failure-mode.description: Backing up the entire server filesystem including .venv, node_modules, and Docker image layers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rebuilding venvs and pulling Docker images is faster than transferring gigabytes of them to B2. Oversized backups exhaust retention budgets faster and increase backup duration.
